gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 review found that compatibility Databricks status evidence accepted non-benchmark g5 statuses and that strict bundles/plans could include compatibility benchmarks without matching compatibility status sidecars. The PR now requires compatibility statuses to be single-task V1 benchmark/vLLM-smoke statuses, rejects storage/engine/other statuses in the compatibility role, and requires matching compatibility Databricks run-status evidence for each strict bundled compatibility benchmark target. The same reviewer rechecked cd75ad4 and approved the fixes.
verification: poetry check && poetry check --lock -> existing Poetry metadata warnings only
poetry run pytest tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_benchmark_plan.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q -> 268 passed
strict release bundle rebuild from the current 23-artifact g5-enriched manifest -> ok=true, artifact_count=23, compatibility_databricks_run_status_16_aws-g5-a10g.json included
GPT-5.5 focused review found two issues; fixes were re-reviewed by the same reviewer and approved
what_changed: Added a compatibility_databricks_run_status release-bundle artifact role and CLI/benchmark-plan plumbing for non-default Databricks compatibility targets.
Required compatibility Databricks status sidecars to be successful non-default V1 benchmark/vLLM-smoke statuses that match bundled compatibility benchmark hardware targets.
Required strict bundles and strict benchmark plans to include matching compatibility Databricks status evidence when compatibility benchmark evidence is bundled.
Updated release docs and governance tests to describe the current 23-artifact g5-enriched strict bundle contract.
why: The g5/A10G compatibility benchmark should travel with release evidence without weakening the strict AWS g6/L4 release target or allowing storage/engine/no-op status sidecars to satisfy benchmark compatibility evidence.
